findings.description: Mixed CJK+Latin queries (e.g. 'cafe拿') use only the CJK length rule, ignoring Latin characters. _meets_reverse_min_length checks cjk_count first; if any CJK chars are present, it requires 2+ CJK regardless of Latin char count. A query like 'cafe拿' (1 CJK + 4 Latin) would fail the guard. This is an edge case unlikely to matter for Taiwan coffee search but deviates slightly from the stated '2+ CJK OR 3+ Latin' rule.
